title: Lesson 17: Input Types (Text, Password, Email, Checkboxes)

content.explanation:
🧾 Understanding Input Types

In the previous lesson, you learned how forms collect data. Now we go deeper into how users actually enter different kinds of data.

HTML provides multiple input types depending on what kind of information you want to collect.

🌐 Why Input Types Matter

Different data needs different controls:

  • Names → text input
  • Emails → email input
  • Passwords → hidden input
  • Choices → checkboxes or radio buttons

syntax_breakdown.content:
🧪 Common Input Types

<input type="text" placeholder="Enter name">
<input type="email" placeholder="Enter email">
<input type="password" placeholder="Enter password">
<input type="checkbox"> Remember me

📌 What Each Does

  • text → normal typing
  • email → validates email format
  • password → hides characters
  • checkbox → yes/no option

🧠 Key Rule

👉 Always choose the correct input type for the data you want to collect

editor_sandbox.starter_code:
Login System

Enter your details below:

mini_challenge.instruction:
🎯 Mission: Build a login form

Inside the <form> tag, add:

1. Text input for username
2. Password input for password
3. Email input
4. A checkbox saying 'Remember me'
5. A submit button

🔥 This is how real login pages start
mini_challenge.hint:
👉 Example:
<input type="text" placeholder="Username">
<input type="password" placeholder="Password">

Make sure all inputs are inside the form

quiz_engine.question: Which input type is used to hide characters when typing sensitive information?
quiz_engine.explanation_feedback: Correct 🎉 The password input type hides characters to protect sensitive user data like login passwords.

takeaways: Different input types are used for different kinds of data
checkbox is used for yes/no choices
